title: Europe by Rail — July 2026 (Milan · Lakes · Bernina · Jungfrau · Paris · Provence · Barcelona · Madrid)

legs.note: Book a morning flight so Como is doable the same afternoon.
Arrival-afternoon day trip to Lake Como.

Trenord direct regionale ~2h20 (trenord.it). Jul 6 is an open northern-lakes buffer (Como/Maggiore/Garda) before the Bernina. Overnight Tirano for the 08:06 Bernina. Marked manual: Italian domestic regional isn't in the open APIs.
BOOKED — Bernina Express, Tirano 08:06 → Chur 12:31, Jul 8. Panoramic, seat reservation held (rhb.ch).

Camping base — iconic Jungfrau valley (72 waterfalls, Staubbach Falls over the campsite). ~3h45 off the Bernina; take the 13:38 (2 chg) not the 13:08 (4 chg). Camping Jungfrau / Schützenbach. Gateway to Wengen/Mürren/Jungfraujoch day trips.
Lauterbrunnen → Paris via Interlaken Ost → Bern → TGV Lyria. ~6.5–7h. Two camping nights (Jul 8, 9) first; Jul 9 = full valley day.
South of France (replaces Amalfi). Direct TGV ~3h15. Base for Provence — Cassis/Calanques, Aix, Avignon day trips. (Swap to Avignon if you prefer villages over a city base.)
Direct TGV ~4h40 (~1–2/day) — the reason Provence beats Nice here. Book early.
AVE ~2.5–3h, frequent. Renfe has no public API — book on renfe.com / Trainline.
Fly home. Trip ends Jul 18 (Bernina on Jul 8 pulled the back half one day earlier).
